content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the left ahead with the car positioned slightly right of center, the left boundary is visible and the car appears to be on course following the curve.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 75, target = (75 + 5) % 121 = 80
- Target WP80: x=1.9924, y=4.4840
- Car: x=2.1474, y=4.5759, heading=158.04°
- dy = 4.4840 - 4.5759 = -0.0919
- dx = 1.9924 - 2.1474 = -0.1550
- bearing = atan2(-0.0919, -0.1550) = 180° + atan(0.0919/0.1550) in third quadrant
- bearing = -(180° - 30.7°) = -149.3°
- delta = -149.3° - 158.04° = -307.34°, normalize: -307.34° + 360° = +52.66°
- |delta| ≈ 52.7°: sharp left turn needed, use steering_angle=+25°, speed=0.75

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves sharply left ahead, car is 0.088m right of center, delta of +52.7° to WP80 indicates a sharp left correction needed, applying +25° steering at 0.75 speed.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Positive steering = left turn, negative = right turn",
    "Delta under 5° means steer 0 to avoid oscillation",
    "Delta 5-15° use ±5°; 15-30° use ±10 to ±15°; 30-50° use ±15 to ±20°; >50° use ±25°",
    "Near WP74-80, track curves sharply left — expect large positive delta, use max left steering",
    "Speed 0.75 needed for sharp turns (|delta|>50°)",
    "Car tends to drift right of center on left curves — anticipate stronger left correction",
    "Heading near 158° corresponds to west-northwest travel on this section",
    "WP80 lookahead gives ~+53° delta at this section — sharp left curve",
    "Oversteer warning: avoid excessive correction when near track center on straights",
    "Track continues curving left through WP75-85 section before straightening"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [2.323, 2.239, 2.147],
    "y": [4.487, 4.529, 4.576],
    "yaw": [163.17, 160.64, 158.04]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP80 at (1.992, 4.484): dy=-0.092, dx=-0.155, bearing=-149.3°, delta=+52.7° → sharp left, steer +25°",
  "speed": 0.75,
  "steering_angle": 25.0
}
```
